'''Maxime Grésèque''' (born 18 March 1981) is a French former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who is currently head coach of the [[Limoux Grizzlies]]. He was a France international representative [[Goal (sport)#Rugby football|goal]]-kicking {{rlp|so}} or {{rlp|sh}}.

He has previously played for [[SM Pia XIII]], [[AS Carcassonne]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the [[Super League]].

Grésèque is the son of former French international and coach, [[Ivan Grésèque]].

==Playing career==
Greseque played from the bench for France against the touring [[Australian Kangaroos]] at the end of the 2005 season, scoring a try.<ref>[https://secure.ausport.gov.au/__data/assets/pdf_file/0007/98638/Australian_Rugby_League_2005.pdf 2005 Annual Report] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090705170103/https://secure.ausport.gov.au/__data/assets/pdf_file/0007/98638/Australian_Rugby_League_2005.pdf |date=5 July 2009 }} – [[Australian Rugby League]] (p. 38)</ref>

He was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#France|France squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="France name official squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/09/24/france-name-official-squad.aspx |title=France name official squad |publisher=RLWC2008 |date=24 September 2008 |accessdate=3 October 2008 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080930072635/http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/09/24/france-name-official-squad.aspx |archivedate=30 September 2008 }}</ref><ref name="France dominated by Dragons">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949078,00.html|title=France dominated by Dragons|publisher=Sky Sports|date=7 August 2008|accessdate=8 August 2008}}</ref>

He was also named in the French national squad for the [[2009 Rugby League Four Nations|2009 Four Nations]], involving the French, English, New Zealand and Australian sides. Grésèque played for France again in the [[2010 European Cup]].
